justification: All pass criteria met: optimistic locking uses expectedCurrentStatus='available', false return throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED, AC3 test simulates race via mock returning false after successful findSeat, AC4 tested with findSeat returning null -> SEAT_NOT_FOUND, confidence section acknowledges the unit-test limitation ('mock assumes updateSeatStatus is truly atomic at the DB layer'), and acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 4 ACs.

observations.text: Three-step pattern (read, fast-path check, atomic update) is well-reasoned — the fast-path avoids unnecessary DB writes while the optimistic lock guards the real race window
AC3 tests include both the race simulation (updateSeatStatus returns false) and verification that the expectedCurrentStatus argument is always passed
AC2 and AC4 tests verify updateSeatStatus is NOT called, showing understanding of early-exit optimization
Confidence section correctly identifies the DB atomicity assumption as the remaining risk area, which satisfies the 'acknowledges AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing' criterion

Confidence section frames the limitation as 'infra concern, not application concern' rather than explicitly stating integration or concurrency tests are needed — slightly hedged but still meets the bar
